seoTitle: How to Merge Accounts in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to merge duplicate accounts in QuickBooks Online so your chart of accounts stays clean and your reports aren't split across near-identical entries.
intro: Duplicate accounts in your chart of accounts can quietly split your data and make reports harder to trust. This guide covers how to merge two accounts in QuickBooks Online into one, so your records stay consolidated and accurate.

steps.heading: Open the chart of accounts
steps.body: Go to your chart of accounts and identify the two accounts you want to combine.

steps.heading: Match the account names exactly
steps.body: Rename one account so its name, detail type, and other key fields exactly match the account you're merging into.

steps.heading: Save the change to trigger the merge
steps.body: When you save the renamed account, QuickBooks Online will prompt you to confirm merging it with the matching account.

steps.heading: Verify the merged transactions
steps.body: Check the surviving account to make sure all historical transactions from both accounts now appear together.

faq.question: Can I undo a merge after it's done?
faq.answer: Merging accounts is permanent, so it's worth double-checking both accounts before confirming the change.

faq.question: Do I lose transaction history when merging accounts?
faq.answer: No, all transactions from both accounts combine under the surviving account rather than being deleted.

faq.question: Why won't QuickBooks let me merge two accounts?
faq.answer: The account type and detail type usually need to match exactly, so check those fields if the merge option isn't appearing.

faq.question: Does merging accounts affect past reports?
faq.answer: Yes, historical reports that reference the merged account will reflect the combined data going forward.
